description: OSMP Pangram Handshake — single canonical demonstration instruction registered as macro. Receiver acquires protocol grammar from one transmission via either deterministic decode (dictionary lookup) or inference-driven single-shot exemplar pattern recognition.

build_provenance.synthesis_method: Theoretical MUST/SHOULD enumeration (STEP0) + ZTOLE Mode 3 panel convergence (STEP1, preliminary 2-lineage) + safety-doctrine completion of non-convergent MUST primitives (target_named, cc_reversible, cc_irreversible)

macros.description: Canonical pangram handshake demonstration instruction. Exercises the essential grammatical primitives of SAL across nine namespaces. Registered under macro_id PANGRAM and reserved by the protocol specification for the pangram handshake purpose per claim 3 of the pangram-handshake claim cluster.
macros.purpose: First-or-among-first handshake message in a bridged session. The first agent transmits this byte-identical canonical demonstration to a second agent in a pre-acquisition state. The second agent, regardless of class (deterministic-decoder or inference-driven), acquires protocol grammar from the single transmission. Receivers verify the canonical form by SHA-256 hash compare to the registered hash above; mismatch causes reversion to pre-acquisition state without applying received content (bounded-infection property, claim 2 of the cluster).
